Tuesday AI Insights 🔴 Live Today AI Strategy UAE Agentic AI Geopolitics

The Week AI Got Honest — And Then the Hardware Wars Started

Anthropic hit $900B. Sam Altman admitted he was wrong. DeepSeek permanently reset what AI should cost. The UAE broke 70.1%. Yesterday Nvidia announced it is now a PC company and Microsoft cut its OpenAI dependency. Today Computex Day 2 and Microsoft Build Day 2 are live. Here is everything verified, dated, and analysed — without the hype.

LW
Lisa Warren
Founder & CEO, Neural Horizons AI  ·  2 June 2026  ·  12 min read
Editorial note on sourcing: This edition covers three time windows, clearly labelled throughout. Sections 01–06 are last week's verified news (25–31 May 2026). Sections 07–08 cover yesterday's major launches (1 June 2026) from Computex and Microsoft Build Day 1. Section 09 covers what is live today, 2 June 2026. Background context from earlier months is used where it adds meaning but is explicitly labelled as such — never as current news.
⬡ Jump to any section
00Key Takeaways — read this first 01Anthropic overtakes OpenAI at $900B+ · Last week 02Altman's "pretty wrong" moment · Last week 03DeepSeek makes the price war permanent · Last week 04UAE 70.1% — both sides of the record · Last week 05Anthropic + Gates Foundation $200M · Last week 06Gemini 3.5 Flash GA · Last week 07Nvidia RTX Spark Superchip · Yesterday 08Microsoft Build: Project Polaris · Yesterday 09🔴 Computex Day 2 & Build Day 2 · Live today 10What to watch this week
⬡ 00 — Key Takeaways · Read this first

Eight things that changed in the last eight days — and what they mean for you

👑
Anthropic is now the world's most valuable private AI company. $30B+ closed at above $900B pre-money the week of 26 May — surpassing OpenAI's March valuation. The pace from $61.5B in March 2025: 14 months.
🎙️
Sam Altman admitted he was "pretty wrong" about AI jobs — four days after OpenAI filed for IPO. Dario Amodei said the same week. Fortune called it a coordinated walk-back timed to capital markets. The truth, as always, sits in the middle.
💥
DeepSeek's 75% V4-Pro price cut went permanent on 22 May. Frontier-grade AI is now 11× cheaper from China than from OpenAI. This is no longer a promotion. It is the new floor — and it changes every AI budget conversation you will have this year.
🇦🇪
The UAE hit 70.1% AI adoption — the first economy on earth to break 70%. Per the Microsoft AI Diffusion Report Q1 2026. Global average: 17.8%. Extraordinary. But the statistic has two sides, and only one of them is good news.
🌍
Anthropic and the Gates Foundation committed $200M to deploy Claude in global health, education, and economic mobility — targeting 4.6 billion people in low- and middle-income countries over four years.
🤖
Gemini 3.5 Flash went GA last week — beating last year's Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agentic benchmarks while running 4× faster. Google's message: Flash is no longer the budget option. Flash is the flagship.
🔴
Yesterday: Nvidia launched the RTX Spark Superchip at Computex. An Arm-based Windows PC chip co-developed with Microsoft — Blackwell GPU, 20-core Grace CPU, 128GB unified memory, 1 petaflop of on-device AI. Dell, HP, Asus, Lenovo, MSI, and Surface devices arriving this autumn. Intel fell 6%. Nvidia is now a PC company.
🔴
Yesterday: Microsoft Build revealed Project Polaris — Microsoft's own in-house AI coding model replacing GPT-4 in GitHub Copilot by August 2026. Windows Agent Framework open-sourced. This is Microsoft designing for a post-OpenAI-dependency future.
⬡ Last Week · Verified 25–31 May 2026

Last week was one of those rare moments when the AI industry paused the performance of confidence and offered something closer to honesty — selectively, carefully, with one eye always on the capital markets. That makes the week more interesting, not less. The job of Tuesday AI Insights is not to amplify the narrative. It is to ask what is actually true, what is being strategically framed, and what it means for leaders building in this region. Let us go.

01 — Anthropic Overtakes OpenAI: $900B, $30B Raised, and an Enterprise Cost Crisis Buried in the Footnotes

✓ Confirmed: week of 26 May 2026

The round closed during the week of 26 May. Sequoia Capital, Dragoneer, Altimeter, and Greenoaks co-led, each contributing approximately $2 billion. Peter Thiel's Founders Fund, General Catalyst, Microsoft, and Nvidia also participated. The total exceeded $30 billion at a pre-money valuation above $900 billion — making Anthropic the world's most valuable private AI company, ahead of OpenAI's March valuation of $852 billion. [Bloomberg / Yahoo Finance]

$900B+
Pre-money valuation at close
$30B
New capital — largest round in Anthropic history
$30B
Annualised revenue run rate, April 2026
14 mo
From $61.5B to $900B+ — pace of ascent

The revenue story behind the valuation is real. Anthropic's annualised run rate rose from $9 billion at end-2025 to approximately $30 billion by early April. Enterprise customers now represent around 80% of revenue, with more than 1,000 businesses spending over $1 million annually. Claude Code drove a significant portion of that growth — which makes the detail that Microsoft quietly cancelled most of its Claude Code licences last week particularly worth sitting with.

⚠ The signal buried in the headline The Verge reported last week that Microsoft cancelled most of its Claude Code licences — redirecting engineers to GitHub Copilot CLI — after per-engineer token bills ran between $500 and $2,000 per month. Uber's COO confirmed the company burned through its entire 2026 AI budget by April with no clear line to improved consumer outcomes. Anthropic's revenue growth is real. So is the cost reckoning happening inside its biggest enterprise customers. [Inc]

"No company in American technology history has grown at this rate. The question is whether the infrastructure built to justify these valuations holds under enterprise cost scrutiny."

— The Next Web, May 2026

02 — Sam Altman Said He Was "Pretty Wrong." Dario Amodei Agreed. Both Have IPOs Coming.

✓ Confirmed: 26 May 2026

On 26 May, speaking at a Commonwealth Bank of Australia conference in Sydney, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told the audience he was "pretty wrong" about AI's economic impact on jobs. He had expected more entry-level white-collar displacement by now. He was "delighted to be wrong." Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei — who once predicted AI could eliminate 50% of white-collar jobs — said the same week that automation may actually expand the work people do. Fortune labelled it a coordinated walk-back. [Fortune, 26 May]

OpenAI filed its S-1 confidentially on 22 May. Altman's reversal came four days later. Anthropic is eyeing its own public listing. Calling this coincidence requires more faith than the evidence supports. [SpaceDaily]

⬡ Seeing both sides

What is true
Yale Budget Lab data through March 2026 shows no meaningful change in unemployment for workers in high AI-exposure occupations. AI is replacing tasks within jobs — not yet entire roles at scale. The apocalypse, so far, has not materialised on the timeline either CEO predicted.
What is also true
Meta laid off 8,000 employees last week and cited AI. A Mercer survey of 825 C-level executives found 99% expect AI to reduce headcount within two years. Agentic AI products are weeks old — the displacement data on autonomous AI agents simply does not exist yet. [Yahoo Finance]

"The organisations using this window to embed AI into operations will be the ones setting the baseline when the curve steepens."

— Lisa Warren, Neural Horizons AI

03 — DeepSeek Made the 75% Price Cut Permanent. This Is Not a Promotion. This Is the New Floor.

✓ Confirmed: 22 May 2026

On 22 May, DeepSeek announced that its promotional discount on V4-Pro — due to expire 31 May — would not roll back. It is now the permanent list price: $0.435 per million input tokens, $0.87 per million output tokens. OpenAI's GPT-5 charges $2.50 input and $10 output. DeepSeek V4-Pro is now 11× cheaper on input, 34× cheaper on output. [The Next Web, 24 May]

–75%
V4-Pro price cut — permanent from 22 May
11×
Cheaper than GPT-5 on input tokens
60%
OpenRouter API traffic already from Chinese models
$0.87
New permanent output price per million tokens

DeepSeek runs on Huawei's Ascend 950 chips — entirely independent of Nvidia. With the Ascend 950 supernode rollout expected in H2 2026, further cuts are structurally probable. DeepSeek does not need venture-scale margin on every call. OpenAI and Anthropic do. That asymmetry is the most important structural fact in AI pricing right now. [CTOL Digital]

⬡ What this means for your AI budget Any AI infrastructure budget built on 2025 pricing is now out of date. If your team routes significant workloads through frontier models, the question of whether lower-cost alternatives can handle parts of those tasks without meaningful quality loss is now a leadership conversation — not just a technical one. This is not a recommendation to switch. It is a prompt to know exactly what you are paying for and why — before your competitors ask the same question.

04 — The UAE Hit 70.1% AI Adoption. Here Is What That Record Confirms — and What It Still Does Not Tell You.

✓ Confirmed: Microsoft AI Diffusion Report Q1 2026, published mid-May 2026

The Microsoft AI Economy Institute's AI Diffusion Report for Q1 2026 placed the UAE first globally: 70.1% of working-age people now use AI tools — the first economy anywhere to cross 70%. The global average is 17.8%. Many countries remain below 10%. [Gulf News]

⬡ Both sides of 70.1%

What the number confirms
The UAE's coordinated approach — UAE AI Strategy, National AI Officer network, mandatory government upskilling — has produced real, measurable penetration. This is not noise. The government-private sector alignment is working as designed. The Stanford AI Index 2026 confirmed the UAE grew AI talent concentration by 121% between 2019 and 2025 — the highest of any country globally.
What it does not confirm
Adoption measures tool use — not changed outcomes, revenue generated, or better decisions made. The UAE's 54% adoption rate earlier this year coexisted with most businesses failing to convert AI usage into measurable outcomes, as Neural Horizons AI reported in May. A higher adoption number makes closing the execution gap more urgent — not less.

The UAE also launched the Cyber Factory — a sovereign AI-driven national cybersecurity initiative — in mid-May, as the Cyber Security Council reported over 800,000 attacks per day. As AI adoption rises, so does the AI-powered attack surface. The two curves are moving together, and they cannot be managed separately. [Computer Weekly]

05 — Anthropic and the Gates Foundation: $200M to Deploy Claude Where the Market Won't Go

✓ Confirmed: 14 May 2026

Anthropic and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation announced a four-year, $200 million partnership — the Gates Foundation's largest AI commitment to date — to deploy Claude across global health, life sciences, education, and economic mobility programmes. Target population: 4.6 billion people in low- and middle-income countries lacking access to essential health services. [Anthropic, 14 May]

◦ What the partnership covers Global health: screening drug and vaccine candidates for neglected diseases including polio, HPV, and eclampsia. Forecast models for malaria and tuberculosis treatment via the Gates Foundation's Institute for Disease Modeling. Education: K-12 tutoring, college advising, literacy and numeracy tools with public benchmarks. Agriculture: crop datasets and benchmarks for smallholder farmers. Economic mobility: portable skills records.

The signal for MENA leaders: the world's most highly-valued AI startup is explicitly investing in non-revenue use cases. That is either genuine mission commitment or sophisticated trust-building — probably both. The discipline of deploying AI in data-constrained, resource-limited environments will teach this industry things that controlled enterprise settings cannot replicate.

06 — Gemini 3.5 Flash: Google Stops Treating Flash as the Budget Option

✓ Launched 19 May at Google I/O · GA confirmed 29 May 2026

Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O on 19 May and confirmed general availability last week. It beats Gemini 3.1 Pro — last year's flagship — on coding and agentic benchmarks while running 4× faster. API pricing: $1.50 input / $9 output per million tokens. It is now the default model in the Gemini app, in Google Search's AI Mode globally, and in the Gemini API. [TechCrunch, 19 May]

⬡ The strategic signal Google no longer treats Flash as a cheap alternative. Flash is the default tier for agentic-first developers. The official I/O framing was explicit: "frontier intelligence with action." Google also launched Managed Agents in the Gemini API — stateful agents running in secure, Google-hosted Linux sandbox environments. The agentic pivot is not coming. It has arrived. Gemini 3.5 Pro — with a 2M-token context window and Deep Think reasoning — is delayed to this month. Sundar Pichai told the I/O audience: "give us until next month." [Google AI Dev Docs]
⬡ Yesterday · 1 June 2026 · Computex & Microsoft Build Day 1

07 — Nvidia Just Became a PC Company: The RTX Spark Superchip Changes the On-Device AI Equation

⬡ Yesterday · 1 June 2026 · Computex 2026, Taipei

In Taipei yesterday, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled the RTX Spark Superchip at Computex 2026 — an Arm-based PC processor co-developed with Microsoft and MediaTek. It combines a Blackwell RTX GPU with 6,144 CUDA cores, a 20-core Grace CPU, up to 128GB of unified memory, and one petaflop of AI performance, built on TSMC's 3nm process. Devices from Dell, HP, Asus, Lenovo, MSI, and Microsoft Surface arrive this autumn. Intel's stock fell 6% on the announcement. [CNBC]

6,144
CUDA cores — Blackwell RTX GPU
128GB
Unified memory — top configuration
1 PFLOP
On-device AI performance
–6%
Intel stock drop on announcement

Huang described it as "the first completely re-engineered, reinvented line of PCs that has happened in 40 years." Adobe is rebuilding Photoshop and Premiere Pro for the RTX Spark architecture. Nvidia also confirmed its Vera CPU is in full production, with early adoption by OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX. [Tom's Hardware]

⬡ What this means for MENA businesses One petaflop of AI performance on-device means a meaningful class of agentic workloads no longer needs to leave the device — or the country. For organisations thinking about data sovereignty, on-premise AI, or reducing cloud dependency, the RTX Spark changes the conversation. Local inference at this scale arriving in mainstream laptops this autumn is not incremental. It is architectural. [MacRumors]

08 — Microsoft Build Day 1: Project Polaris Revealed — Microsoft Is Building Its Own AI to Replace GPT-4 in Copilot

⬡ Yesterday · 1 June 2026 · Microsoft Build, San Francisco

The most significant announcement at Build 2026's opening day had nothing to do with Windows. Microsoft unveiled Project Polaris — its own in-house AI coding model, set to replace GPT-4 Turbo as the default model for GitHub Copilot subscribers from August 2026, with automatic migration and a three-month optional fallback period. Microsoft also open-sourced the Windows Agent Framework, announced the Azure Agent Mesh, and shipped Copilot Workspace out of beta. [ChatForest recap]

⬡ Both sides of Microsoft's OpenAI independence move

What it means for Microsoft
Project Polaris gives Microsoft cost control, IP ownership, and strategic independence from OpenAI — whose IPO will fundamentally change the commercial relationship. Having cancelled most Claude Code licences last week and now building its own model, Microsoft is designing for a post-OpenAI-dependency future. Agent 365, its enterprise AI governance platform, went GA on 1 May and now sits underneath all of this.
What it means for everyone else
GitHub Copilot had a 9% developer love score in the JetBrains April survey — against 46% for Claude Code. If Polaris does not close that gap quickly, Microsoft's developer credibility takes a lasting hit. The open-sourcing of the Windows Agent Framework directly challenges Google's Antigravity and Anthropic's agent APIs. The agentic OS wars are now open and competitive. [Notebookcheck]
🔴 Live Today · 2 June 2026 · Computex Day 2 & Microsoft Build Day 2

09 — Live Today: Intel Enters the AI GPU Race, Surface Laptop Ultra Revealed, Jensen Huang at Build

🔴 Live: 2 June 2026 · Computex Day 2, Taipei + Microsoft Build Day 2, San Francisco

Computex 2026 officially opened today, 2 June, running through 5 June in Taipei under the theme "AI Together." Microsoft Build Day 2 runs simultaneously in San Francisco. The two events together make today one of the most significant days in hardware and developer AI news this year. [Tom's Hardware live]

🔴 Breaking from Computex today Intel unveils Crescent Island AI GPU Intel has unveiled its long-awaited Crescent Island data-centre GPU — built on Xe3P architecture, featuring up to 480GB of LPDDR5X memory, and explicitly positioned as "built for agentic AI." Full raw compute specs have not yet been released. This is Intel's direct answer to Nvidia's data-centre dominance and its most significant AI GPU announcement in several years. [Tom's Hardware, 2 June]
🔴 Breaking from Computex today Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra revealed Microsoft has unveiled the Surface Laptop Ultra — the most direct MacBook Pro competitor the company has ever shipped. It carries Nvidia's RTX Spark Superchip: 20-core CPU, Blackwell GPU, 128GB of unified RAM, housed in a 15-inch chassis with a mini-LED display, HDMI, USB-C, USB-A, and SD card reader. [Tom's Hardware, 2 June]
🔴 Live: Microsoft Build Day 2 Jensen Huang is joining the Build stage today — Computex and Microsoft Build overlapping in real time. Day 2 sessions focus on Azure AI Foundry enterprise integrations, multi-agent orchestration, and the developer SDK for Windows on-device AI. AMD's Radeon RX 9070 GRE also goes global today at $549.

10 — What to Watch This Week

⬡ On the radar — week of 2 June 2026

Today
Microsoft Build Day 2 + Computex Day 2 — Jensen Huang on stage at Build. Intel Crescent Island GPU and Surface Laptop Ultra both announced this morning. Watch the Azure AI Foundry cost governance sessions and the developer SDK for Windows AI unification. [Microsoft Build Live]
3 June
Neural Horizons AI Webinar — Live executive briefing with Lisa Warren. Closing the UAE execution gap: from adoption to outcomes. Register free →
2–5 June
Computex 2026 continues through Friday. AMD Nova Lake CPU teasers expected. Qualcomm's response to Nvidia's RTX Spark is live. Watch the budget PC segment closely — Apple's MacBook Neo is forcing every OEM to compete on value and AI capability simultaneously.
June 2026
Gemini 3.5 Pro GA expected — Sundar Pichai said "give us until next month" at I/O. A 2M-token context window and Deep Think reasoning targeting Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 head-to-head. When it lands, the frontier pricing conversation resets again.
8–12 June
Apple WWDC 2026 — New CEO John Ternus must show Siri 2.0 working as a genuine on-screen AI agent. Apple's on-device strategy now has direct hardware competition from Nvidia's RTX Spark and Intel's Crescent Island. If WWDC delivers, the consumer AI landscape shifts significantly.
30 June
Colorado AI Act takes effect — first US state-level comprehensive AI regulation on high-risk systems. If your business serves US customers with AI-powered decision-making, compliance planning is no longer optional.
24 July
DeepSeek alias retirement deadline — any production code calling legacy deepseek-chat or deepseek-reasoner aliases must migrate to deepseek-v4-pro or deepseek-v4-flash before 24 July or face API failures.

Last week's AI story changed the pricing floor, the valuation ceiling, the hardware landscape, and the cost of doing nothing. The question for every leader in this region is not whether to move — it is whether your AI strategy is built to convert adoption into competitive advantage before the market does it for you.
